details: The "Mukhymantri Sarthi Yojana" was launched by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training and Skill Development, Government of Jharkhand. The scheme aims to provide industry-relevant skill development training for youth in Jharkhand, aligning with NSQF standards. Implemented by JSDMS across all 24 districts, it offers free training to enhance employability and self-employment opportunities. The scheme ensures quality training with a focus on marginalized communities, including SC/ST, women, PwD, and transgender individuals.
benefits: Industry-aligned, high-quality skill development training at no cost.
Includes soft skills and IT training for better employability.
Uniforms, participant handbooks, and required tools for free.
Rozgar Protsahan Bhatta ₹1,000/month for boys and ₹1,500/month for girls, transgender, and PwD for one year if unemployed after training.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Jharkhand.
The applicant's age should be 18-35 years.
The applicant must belong to one of the priority groups (SC/ST, women, PwD, transgender).

online: for registration under the "Mukhymantri Sarthi Yojna" scheme.
Candidate registration would be Aadhaar-based & hence Aadhaar number at the time of candidate registration would be mandatory.

faqs.question: What is Mukhymantri Sarthi Yojna?
faqs.answer: It is a skill development scheme to provide free NSQF-aligned training for employability.

faqs.question: Who can apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Residents of Jharkhand aged 18-35 years (50 years for SC/ST under BIRSA).

faqs.question: Is the training free of cost?
faqs.answer: Yes, the training is completely free of cost.

faqs.question: What is Rozgar Protsahan Bhatta?
faqs.answer: An allowance of ₹1000/month for boys and ₹1500/month for girls, transgender, and PwD for one year if unemployed.

faqs.question: What type of training is provided?
faqs.answer: Industry-relevant, NSQF-aligned training, including soft skills and IT.

faqs.question: Are there residential facilities?
faqs.answer: Yes, both residential and non-residential training centers are available.

faqs.question: Can non-residential trainees get transport allowance?
What are the materials provided?
faqs.answer: Uniforms, participant handbooks, and other essentials.

faqs.question: How to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Visit your district training center and fill out the application with required documents.

faqs.question: What happens if I remain unemployed after the training?
faqs.answer: You will receive Rozgar Protsahan Bhatta for up to one year.

faqs.question: What is the role of NSDC?
faqs.answer: NSDC accredits training centers under SMART measures for quality assurance.

faqs.question: Is IT training mandatory for all courses?
faqs.answer: Yes, IT training is included to enhance holistic development.
